---
title: "Locations of sampling areas"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r, install packages}

#install.packages(c("ggplot2", "sf", "ggmap", "ggspatial"))
#install.packages("leaflet")
library(leaflet)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}


library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)

# Load data
usgs_locations <- as.data.frame(read_excel("C:/Users/savan/OneDrive - San Francisco State University/ProjectData/GitHub/DepositionSFB/data/Locations Karen's Data.xlsx"))
thesis_locations <- as.data.frame(read_csv("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\All_Sites_Calculated.csv"))

# Convert UTM to lat/lon (USGS)
#UTM Zone 10N (WGS84)
coords_sf_usgs <- st_as_sf(usgs_locations,
                            coords = c("Easting", "Northing"),
                            crs = 32610)
coords_wgs84_usgs <- st_transform(coords_sf_usgs, crs = 4326) 
coords_matrix_usgs <- st_coordinates(coords_wgs84_usgs)
usgs_locations$Longitude <- coords_matrix_usgs[, 1]
usgs_locations$Latitude  <- coords_matrix_usgs[, 2]
usgs_locations$Site <- sub("\\s.*", "", usgs_locations$ID)

# Convert UTM to lat/lon (Thesis) — fixed to use coords_sf_thesis
#California State Plane Zone 3 (NAD83) 
coords_sf_thesis <- st_as_sf(thesis_locations,
                              coords = c("Easting", "Northing"),
                              crs = 2227)  # ← Zone 3, US feet
coords_wgs84_thesis <- st_transform(coords_sf_thesis, crs = 4326)
coords_matrix_thesis <- st_coordinates(coords_wgs84_thesis)
thesis_locations$Longitude <- coords_matrix_thesis[, 1]
thesis_locations$Latitude  <- coords_matrix_thesis[, 2]

# Check the RANGE this time, not just the first few rows
summary(thesis_locations[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")])

# Combined map
ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = usgs_locations,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Site),
             size = 3, shape = 16) +
  geom_text(data = usgs_locations,
            aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, label = ID),
            vjust = -0.8, size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(data = thesis_locations,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = Transect_ID),
             size = 3, shape = 17) +          # different shape to distinguish
  geom_text(data = thesis_locations,
            aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, label = Transect_ID),  # ← replace with your label column
            vjust = -0.8, size = 2.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Site Locations",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude",
       color = "Site / Transect")
```

```{r}
# Create the Transect column
usgs_locations$Transect <- sub(".* ([A-D]).*", "\\1", usgs_locations$ID)

# Color scheme for USGS transects
transect_colors <- colorFactor(
  palette = c("blue", "red", "green", "orange"),
  domain = c("A", "B", "C", "D")
)

# color scheme for thesis transects 

thesis_colors <- colorFactor(
  palette = c("purple", "cyan", "pink", "black"),
  domain = unique(thesis_locations$Transect_ID)
)

leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  

  # --- USGS layer ---
  addCircleMarkers(
    data = usgs_locations,
    lng = ~Longitude,
    lat = ~Latitude,
    color = ~transect_colors(Transect),
    fillColor = ~transect_colors(Transect),
    radius = 6,
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    label = ~ID,
    popup = ~paste(
      "ID:", ID,
      "<br>Transect:", Transect,
      "<br>Easting:", Easting,
      "<br>Northing:", Northing
    ),
    group = "USGS"
  ) %>%
  
  # --- Thesis layer ---
  addCircleMarkers(
    data = thesis_locations,
    lng = ~Longitude,
    lat = ~Latitude,
    color = ~thesis_colors(Transect_ID),
    fillColor = ~thesis_colors(Transect_ID),
    stroke = TRUE,
    radius = 6,
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    opacity = 0.8,
    label = ~Transect_ID,
    popup = ~paste(
      "Transect:", Transect_ID,
      "<br>Easting:", Easting,
      "<br>Northing:", Northing
    ),
    group = "Thesis"
  ) %>%
  
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = transect_colors,
    values = usgs_locations$Transect,
    title = "USGS Transect"
  ) %>%

  addLegend(
    position = "bottomleft",
    pal = thesis_colors,
    values = thesis_locations$Transect_ID,
    title = "Thesis Transect",
    group = "Thesis"
  )
```
